symbols.signature: function checkUrl(url: string, timeoutMs = 5000): Promise<{ latencyMs: number; ok: boolean }>
symbols.type_text: (url: string, timeoutMs?: number) => Promise<{ latencyMs: number; ok: boolean }>

symbols.signature: export async function getSession(cookieHeader: string | null): Promise<UserSession | null>
export async function persistError(entry: ErrorLogEntry): Promise<void>
symbols.source: read — reference pattern for Partial 2's service_health_checks insert function (lazy dedicated pg.Pool, best-effort catch, no throw)

api_contracts.request_type: none — auth via `cookie` request header (workspace_session), no body/query params
api_contracts.response_type: 200: { results: Record<string, ServiceCheck[]>; checkedAt: string } where ServiceCheck = { name: string; slug: string; url: string; status: 'ok'|'slow'|'error'|'optional'; latencyMs: number|null; optional: boolean; error?: string } — keyed by BRAND_ID/BRAND cluster name, filtered to assets with non-empty health_url whose clusters[] includes the current cluster. 401: { error: 'Unauthorized' } when no session or !isAdmin(session). 503: { error: string } when listSoftwareAssets() throws (DB unreachable).

external_types.signature: export type Plugin = (input: PluginInput, options?: PluginOptions) => Promise<Hooks>
external_types.source: .opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts (local .d.ts, read directly — package IS present under .opencode/node_modules)

external_types.signature: "tool.execute.before"?: (input: { tool: string; sessionID: string; callID: string }, output: { args: any }) => Promise<void>

event?: (input: { event: Event }) => Promise<void> — there is NO dedicated 'session.idle'/'session.deleted' hook key in Hooks; those are dispatched through this generic `event` hook and discriminated via `event.type` (confirmed live precedent: ~/.config/opencode/plugins/loop-guard.ts lines 108-113)
ingest_traces(params: { traces: object[]; project: string }) — both fields required, no further param-level typing exposed by the tool schema

risks.note: otel-collector root cause is not yet diagnosed (only the symptom — 9 boot lines in 25 days, no payload — is confirmed live per design.md). Partial 1 must diagnose (factory-pipeline call site, endpoint config, NetworkPolicy) before it can fix; effort is not yet bounded.

Task brief named the log-explorer dashboard file as 'k3d/monitoring/grafana-dashboard-log-explorer.yaml'; the real path is 'k3d/monitoring/grafana-dashboards/log-explorer.json' (plural 'dashboards' dir, .json not .yaml). Corrected in impact_files; the spec (centralized-logging.md) also requires the sibling 'api-errors.json' dashboard, which was added alongside it.

Promtail's exact brand-relabel rule order/semantics (which rule wins, action: replace vs keep) is explicitly flagged in design.md as unverified against the live config — the plan-subagent must re-verify against k3d/monitoring/promtail-rendered.yaml's live-applied ConfigMap before writing the exact correction; this intel bundle only pre-computes the file's S1 budget, not the relabel fix itself.

service_health_checks / service_health_goals (Partial 2) do not exist yet — db_tables only contains the live-queried FK target platform.software_assets (PK: id uuid, UNIQUE: slug). The new tables' exact DDL must still be authored by the plan-subagent per specs/service-health-goals.md; not independently verified against a live migration.

Migration filename 'website/src/db/migrations/20260724_create_service_health.sql' is a suggested placeholder following the repo's observed <YYYYMMDD>_description.sql convention (e.g. 20260719_add_missing_fk_indexes_batch2.sql) — the plan-subagent must confirm the actual date at write time, not reuse this literal filename if it is stale.

No call_graph edges were added for the not-yet-existing Partial 2/3 files (health-goals.ts, agent-tracer.ts) since codebase-memory/trace_path cannot trace symbols that don't exist yet; their intended data flow (CronJob → trigger route → checkUrl → insert; tool.execute.before → accumulate → event(session.idle/deleted) → ingest_traces) is documented narratively in design.md instead.
